author: @lakshmivariar
text: hi sir i m doing a project of making transparent electrode of Graphene  but on reducing GO i m getting a metallic luster which is restricting transparency.can u please tell me why that luster and is there any way to avoid it?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yo're making your film too thick - the metallic luster is graphite

replies.author: @lakshmivariar
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith i m dipcoating GO on PVDF film and coating thickness is like a few microns maximum.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
lakshmi variar
why not mix the two and do in situ reduction using UV?

replies.author: @lakshmivariar
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith i need an insulator with conducting coating on both sides

author: @elnazhamidi4166
text: Hi.im working on a project which is related to graphene.i have to reduce graphene oxide electrochemically.have you worked on it?i really need help.

author: @munibzain1966
text: I want a full way to get graphene

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: did you look at the other videos? - i have done loads of ways to get graphene

author: @1picotin
text: Thank you for your very interesting videos. I suggest an idea, probably very naive and certainly stupid..:  in order to reduce graphene oxide to graphene do  you think it would be possible to make a cathode with aluminium or titanium coated withe graphene oxide protected with microporous varnish and running this cathode in a mild electrolyte like sodium sulfate, at this cathode would be produced nascent hydrogen that could possibly reduce the graphene oxide to graphene leaving thus a titanium or aluminium sheet or ord coated with graphene ?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think it is more than probable that this idea would work - nice thinking

author: @TheIdeanator
text: Is there an easy way to produce GO/graphene from wood/paper/random carbon source instead of graphite?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes but it is long winded
